key_insight: A lightweight alignment layer can connect frozen histopathology and RNA-seq foundation models, enabling H&E slides to be queried with gene-set signatures to predict molecular pathway activity without direct sequencing.
clinical_relevance: This could help infer clinically relevant tumor programs and microenvironment states from routine pathology slides, supporting subtype and biomarker-style stratification when RNA testing is unavailable or impractical.
method: Freeze histopathology and RNA-seq foundation models
Train a small contrastive alignment module on paired multimodal data
Use open-vocabulary gene-set prompts on H&E slides to estimate pathway activity
evidence_signal: In a multi-cancer cohort of 1,720 cases, the approach achieved a 25-fold retrieval improvement over baselines and showed strongest predictability for morphologically grounded programs, with external clinical validation on the POSEIDON trial.
watch_item: Predictions are strongest for pathways with clear morphological correlates, so programs lacking visible tissue signatures may remain unreliable and require careful validation before use.
paper_title: Data-Efficient Multimodal Alignment for Histopathology-based Molecular Prediction
